title: CRISP-DM in Public Programme Delivery Pipelines
summary: How I adapt CRISP-DM phases to match institutional timelines and stakeholder decision windows.
content: This note breaks down how CRISP-DM can be tuned for real-world programme delivery, especially when multiple stakeholders need answers at different times.
I highlight where discovery, data preparation, and evaluation should be compressed or extended to match operational decision windows.
The goal is to keep governance intact while still moving quickly enough to inform action.

title: Choosing Between Descriptive and Predictive Workflows
summary: Decision criteria for selecting fit-for-purpose analysis depth under real operational constraints.
content: Some problems require only clear descriptive insight, while others need full predictive modelling.
This note explains how to decide between the two based on uncertainty, time pressure, and available data quality.
I share a short decision framework used in delivery work to avoid over-engineering.

title: Data Quality Checks Before Dashboard Delivery
summary: A practical checklist for auditability, validation, and source consistency before publishing dashboards.
content: Before any dashboard goes live, I run a short quality checklist that protects stakeholder trust.
This includes source validation, reconciliation against official figures, and checks for missing or biased fields.
The checklist is lightweight but strong enough for audit-ready delivery.

title: Prompt Governance for AI-Assisted Analytics Teams
summary: Prompt standards and review controls that maintain quality when teams adopt generative AI tools.
content: As teams scale AI-assisted analytics, prompt quality becomes a governance issue.
This note outlines prompt standards, review checkpoints, and lightweight QA protocols.
The approach keeps creativity while protecting data integrity and decision quality.
